name: Smallstep Certificate Manager API
what_it_does: Automated certificate management platform with REST API — providing private PKI-as-a-service for issuing, rotating, and revoking TLS/mTLS certificates at scale, supporting ACME, OIDC, and SSH certificate provisioning for zero-trust infrastructure.
use_cases: Issuing short-lived TLS certificates to services for mTLS authentication via REST API
Automating certificate rotation with ACME protocol for internal services
SSH certificate provisioning for ephemeral access without long-lived SSH keys
Private CA management for Kubernetes, service mesh, and zero-trust network environments
OIDC-based certificate issuance tying TLS identity to user/service identity provider
not_for: Public-facing TLS certificates for browsers (use Let's Encrypt or DigiCert)
Teams without PKI knowledge — Smallstep requires understanding of certificate lifecycles
Simple self-signed certificate generation without lifecycle management
best_when: An infrastructure team needs automated private PKI for mTLS, zero-trust networking, and short-lived certificates with ACME support and OIDC integration.
avoid_when: You need publicly trusted certificates for websites — Smallstep is for internal/private PKI.

auth.notes: OIDC tokens for human certificate issuance. JWK provisioner for machine identity. ACME protocol for automated renewal. Smallstep Cloud uses API keys. Self-hosted step-ca uses provisioner-based auth. mTLS for service-to-service.

pricing.free_tier_limits: step-ca: Apache 2.0 — completely free; Smallstep Cloud: free up to 50 team members
pricing.paid_tiers: Smallstep Cloud from $300/month for 50+ members; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: step-ca is Apache 2.0 open source — free forever self-hosted. Smallstep Cloud is managed PKI. US-based, well-funded startup. Strong enterprise adoption for zero-trust. step CLI for automation. ACME server included.

performance.rate_limits: Certificate issuance rate limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for certificate management (issue, renew, revoke). ACME for automated renewal. OIDC provisioner for identity-based issuance. step CLI for automation scripting. Good documentation with practical examples. Webhook support for certificate events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Certificate issuance creates new cert each call — use renewal for rotation. Revocation idempotent (already revoked = 200). Certificate queries idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Certificate issuance requires provisioner configuration — not plug-and-play
Short-lived certs (default 24h) require automated renewal — build rotation into agent
ACME provisioner requires DNS/HTTP challenge for identity verification
Self-hosted step-ca requires root CA backup — loss is unrecoverable

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable Go codebase. Mutual TLS by design. OIDC integration for identity-bound certificates. Short-lived certificates limit exposure window. SOC2 certified (cloud). Hardware security module (HSM) support for root key protection. Zero-trust native architecture.
